title: Integral Floer Homology Theory
speaker: Guangbo Xu
abstract: (joint work with Shaoyun Bai) Using a new version of transversality condition (the FOP transversality) on orbifolds, one can construct Hamiltonian Floer theory over integers for all compact symplectic manifolds. In this talk I will first describe the formal structures in this theory, including the formalism of flow categories, bimodules, homotopies, their lifts to more refined categories such as derived orbifolds. The FOP perturbation method allows us to deduce from such formal structures the usual objects in Floer theory: the Floer chain complex, continuation map, PSS map, pair-of-pants product, Steenrod-type operations, etc. I will mention two dynamical applications for any compact symplectic manifold: 1) the existence of a non-contractible periodic orbit of a Hamiltonian implies infinitely many such orbits; 2) the existence of Hamiltonian pseudorotation implies geometric uniruledness, i.e., there exists a rational curve through each point.

proof_stages.label: Pseudo-manifold target for integral counts
proof_stages.summary: The virtual-category recipe is redirected from weighted multivalued counts toward pseudo-manifolds, whose codimension-at-least-two boundary strata do not affect zero-dimensional counts and hence can support an integral Floer differential.

proof_stages.label: Derived-orbifold charts and zero loci
proof_stages.summary: A derived orbifold is introduced as a triple consisting of an orbifold, an orbifold vector bundle, and a continuous section, modeling finite-dimensional reductions of pseudoholomorphic-curve moduli spaces and carrying a natural zero-locus functor to topological spaces.

proof_stages.label: Morphisms by stabilization and open embedding
proof_stages.summary: The relevant morphisms between derived orbifolds are restricted to maps that factor through stabilization by a disk bundle followed by an open embedding, reflecting enlargement of obstruction spaces and shrinking of Kuranishi neighborhoods.

proof_stages.label: Normal complex structures as the integrality hypothesis
proof_stages.summary: Because ordinary single-valued transverse perturbations need not exist on orbifolds, the speaker introduces normal complex structures on the nontrivial stabilizer-representation directions of tangent and obstruction bundles, a condition naturally supplied in Floer theory by the complex symbol of the Cauchy-Riemann operator and sphere bubbling.

proof_stages.label: FOP transverse perturbations produce pseudo-manifolds
proof_stages.summary: The FOP black-box theorem provides a C0-dense class of single-valued perturbing sections that are transverse on manifold points, stable under stabilization and products, and whose manifold-part zero sets close up to pseudo-manifolds.

proof_stages.label: AMS lift and integral Floer complex
proof_stages.summary: The AMS construction lifts the Hamiltonian Floer category to normally complex derived orbifolds, and composing this lift with FOP perturbations and pseudo-manifold counts yields a chain complex over the Novikov ring with integer coefficients and hence integral Floer homology.

proof_stages.label: Finite-characteristic operations and dynamical applications
proof_stages.summary: Once integer counts are available, the theory can be reduced modulo primes to use quantum Steenrod operations, leading to compact-symplectic applications such as infinitely many simple periodic orbits from a noncontractible one-periodic orbit and geometric uniruledness for pseudo-rotations.

examples.name: Product local model for corners
examples.note: In response to a question about stratifications, the speaker says the allowed corner behavior is locally a product of a corner-free object with a standard corner, illustrating the controlled stratified setting for zero loci.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker only describes corners as locally a product with a standard corner to clarify the class of stratified objects, without using or analyzing the model further

examples.name: Tangent-bundle fixed-locus normal bundle
examples.note: Replacing the obstruction bundle by the tangent bundle makes the nontrivial stabilizer-representation part exactly the normal bundle to the fixed-point locus, making the definition of normal complex structure concrete.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker restricts to an H-fixed locus, decomposes the tangent representation into trivial and nontrivial parts, and identifies the nontrivial tangent part with the normal bundle

passes.reason: The normal bundle to a fixed locus inside the tangent bundle is a standard equivariant-geometric construction illustrating normal complex structures, not a sporadic, limiting, or extremal object.

examples.name: Semi-positive Gromov-Witten comparison
examples.note: An audience question prompts the speaker to compare the construction with the familiar semi-positive, genus-zero stable range where the resulting integer invariants are expected to agree with usual Gromov-Witten invariants.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker only states an expected agreement of the integral construction with usual genus-zero semi-positive Gromov-Witten invariants in the stable range, without carrying out a comparison
